finding.title: C1+C5a: persist host capability handshake on RemediationState; drop report-overwrite warning
finding.summary: Files: src/remediate/index.ts, src/remediate/steps/nextStep.ts, src/remediate/state/store.ts. Add a host_capabilities field to RemediationState (store.ts ~18-48) round-tripped by StateStore.mutate; validateState must accept it. In resolveHostDispatchCapability (nextStep.ts ~126-190), resolve each capability field as explicitFlag ?? persistedField ?? floor (per-field merge — commander leaves omitted --host-* options undefined, so omitted is distinguishable from explicit); persist the EXPLICITLY-supplied fields back to state on any call that supplies them (fill-the-gap, override-dominant; corrupt/absent persisted value degrades to flags-or-floor, never throws). True first contact (no persisted, no flags) keeps the 32k floor unchanged. Remove the report-overwrite warning process.stderr.write at nextStep.ts ~3905 (C5a). Tests: persisted handshake reused when omitted; per-field override; warning absent; first-contact floored; corrupt degrades.
finding.concrete_change: Files: src/remediate/index.ts, src/remediate/steps/nextStep.ts, src/remediate/state/store.ts. Add a host_capabilities field to RemediationState (store.ts ~18-48) round-tripped by StateStore.mutate; validateState must accept it. In resolveHostDispatchCapability (nextStep.ts ~126-190), resolve each capability field as explicitFlag ?? persistedField ?? floor (per-field merge — commander leaves omitted --host-* options undefined, so omitted is distinguishable from explicit); persist the EXPLICITLY-supplied fields back to state on any call that supplies them (fill-the-gap, override-dominant; corrupt/absent persisted value degrades to flags-or-floor, never throws). True first contact (no persisted, no flags) keeps the 32k floor unchanged. Remove the report-overwrite warning process.stderr.write at nextStep.ts ~3905 (C5a). Tests: persisted handshake reused when omitted; per-field override; warning absent; first-contact floored; corrupt degrades.

finding.title: D2: rejected-artifact archive keeps original path re-authorable + signposted message
finding.summary: File: src/remediate/steps/contractPipeline.ts ONLY. archiveContractArtifact (~291-303) moves the rejected artifact to history/<name>.<label>-<ts>.json (retained for audit) so the ORIGINAL path is free; ensure the rejection re-emit messages (~1300/1306/1497) explicitly state the prior output was archived (to <history path>) and instruct a full rewrite (Write, not Edit). If the history/ move fails, preserve the original at its path (degrade, never silently drop). Tests: after reject+archive, a fresh Write to the original path succeeds; the re-emit message contains the archive signpost; the history copy exists; a failed history move preserves the original.
finding.concrete_change: File: src/remediate/steps/contractPipeline.ts ONLY. archiveContractArtifact (~291-303) moves the rejected artifact to history/<name>.<label>-<ts>.json (retained for audit) so the ORIGINAL path is free; ensure the rejection re-emit messages (~1300/1306/1497) explicitly state the prior output was archived (to <history path>) and instruct a full rewrite (Write, not Edit). If the history/ move fails, preserve the original at its path (degrade, never silently drop). Tests: after reject+archive, a fresh Write to the original path succeeds; the re-emit message contains the archive signpost; the history copy exists; a failed history move preserves the original.

finding.title: C5b: suppress worktree-remove noise only for the already-absent case via path-existence precheck
finding.summary: File: src/remediate/steps/dispatch.ts ONLY. In removeWorktree (~610), precheck whether the worktree path exists / is a registered working tree BEFORE running git worktree remove; if it is already absent, treat as a no-op success and write nothing to stderr (OS/locale-agnostic — do NOT match on the 'not a working tree' stderr text). A genuine, unexpected removal failure (path exists but removal fails for another reason) is still logged/surfaced exactly as today. Tests: removeWorktree on an absent path -> no stderr, no throw; a non-absent removal failure -> still surfaced.
finding.concrete_change: File: src/remediate/steps/dispatch.ts ONLY. In removeWorktree (~610), precheck whether the worktree path exists / is a registered working tree BEFORE running git worktree remove; if it is already absent, treat as a no-op success and write nothing to stderr (OS/locale-agnostic — do NOT match on the 'not a working tree' stderr text). A genuine, unexpected removal failure (path exists but removal fails for another reason) is still logged/surfaced exactly as today. Tests: removeWorktree on an absent path -> no stderr, no throw; a non-absent removal failure -> still surfaced.

finding.title: C4: single-source the obligation-membership predicate shared by both scaffolds
finding.summary: File: src/remediate/contractPipeline/derive.ts ONLY (in-scope). Extract ONE shared predicate/function (off TESTABLE_KINDS) deciding obligation-kind membership, and have BOTH buildTestValidatorPlanScaffold (~173-187) and buildImplementationDagScaffold (~232-268) derive their obligation set from it: testable (invariant/behavioral) -> both phases; structural OBL-<module>-contract -> implementation_dag only (covered by validateImplementationDAGIntegrity, never required in the test plan); unknown kind -> conservatively treated as testable (must appear in test plan, no silent drop). Add a drift lock-test asserting the two scaffolds use the same predicate and a structural obligation never lands in the test plan nor drops from the DAG, while a testable obligation missing from the test plan still fails the paired/coverage gate. NOTE (residual, out-of-scope): a separate TESTABLE_KINDS duplicate exists in contractPipelineGates.ts ~438 — do NOT edit gates.ts this node (out of declared write scope); leave a code comment in derive.ts noting the gates.ts duplicate as a follow-up unification.
finding.concrete_change: File: src/remediate/contractPipeline/derive.ts ONLY (in-scope). Extract ONE shared predicate/function (off TESTABLE_KINDS) deciding obligation-kind membership, and have BOTH buildTestValidatorPlanScaffold (~173-187) and buildImplementationDagScaffold (~232-268) derive their obligation set from it: testable (invariant/behavioral) -> both phases; structural OBL-<module>-contract -> implementation_dag only (covered by validateImplementationDAGIntegrity, never required in the test plan); unknown kind -> conservatively treated as testable (must appear in test plan, no silent drop). Add a drift lock-test asserting the two scaffolds use the same predicate and a structural obligation never lands in the test plan nor drops from the DAG, while a testable obligation missing from the test plan still fails the paired/coverage gate. NOTE (residual, out-of-scope): a separate TESTABLE_KINDS duplicate exists in contractPipelineGates.ts ~438 — do NOT edit gates.ts this node (out of declared write scope); leave a code comment in derive.ts noting the gates.ts duplicate as a follow-up unification.
